chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P. No. S--90 of 2018. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 09.02.2018. FOR ORDERS ON OFFICE OBJECTIONS. FOR KATCHA PESHI. Petitioner Mst. Kenjhar present along with her counsel Mr. Ali Khan Leghari. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, A.A.G. along with ASI Muzafar Ali Khoso I/C. S.H.O. Police Station Waleed District Larkana and Inspector/SHO P.S. Maya District Tando Muhammad Khan on behalf of SSP Tando Muhammad Khan. Syed Meeral Shah Bukhari, A.P.G. Petitioner present in Court states that she being sui juris has contracted marriage with Nadeem Ali out of her free will and wish and leading a happy life with her husband. Regarding such marriage ‘free will affidavit’ and ‘Nikahnama’ both dated 11.01.2018 are also available on record. Further she states that she has neither been abducted nor otherwise pressurized by her husband Nadeem Ali who is also present in court. She further states that she is living together as husband and wife but due to this unceremonial marriage, her parents and her other relatives are not happy and they are harassing her as well as her husband with collusion of police officials. Learned A.A.G. files comments of respondents No.2 to 5, taken on record. Copy whereof has been supplied to the counsel for the petitioner in which it is denied that the official respondents are harassing the petitioners. However, learned A.A.G. and A.P.G. present in Court submit that the official respondents would act strictly in accordance with law. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with direction to the official respondents to make sure that there should not be caused any harassment to the petitioners.

However, learned A.A.G. and A.P.G. present in Court submit that the official respondents would act strictly in accordance with law. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with direction to the official respondents to make sure that there should not be caused any harassment to the petitioners. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE A.
